---
title: "Estimators.2"
author: "Cheng Ma"
date: "2026.4.14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
##Estimator 2: Mean Personal Income of Michiganders

##For Estimator 2, we estimate the mean personal income of Michiganders using the 2023 Michigan ACS PUMS person-level data. The variable of interest is PINCP, and the auxiliary variable is AGEP. Missing values of PINCP and AGEP are excluded, while zero and negative income values are retained.

##We use a regression estimator as the primary estimator and a ratio estimator for comparison. As in Estimator 1, the bound on the error of estimation is defined as

##Bound=2×SE.
##Read and Merge Data
library(haven)

tf_household <- tempfile()
this_url_household <-
  "https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data/pums/2023/1-Year/sas_hmi.zip"
download.file(this_url_household, tf_household, mode = "wb")
unzipped_files_household <- unzip(tf_household, exdir = tempdir())
acs_sas_household <- grep("\\.sas7bdat$", unzipped_files_household, value = TRUE)
acs_df_household <- read_sas(acs_sas_household)
names(acs_df_household) <- tolower(names(acs_df_household))

tf_person <- tempfile()
this_url_person <-
  "https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data/pums/2023/1-Year/sas_pmi.zip"
download.file(this_url_person, tf_person, mode = "wb")
unzipped_files_person <- unzip(tf_person, exdir = tempdir())
acs_sas_person <- grep("\\.sas7bdat$", unzipped_files_person, value = TRUE)
acs_df_person <- read_sas(acs_sas_person)
names(acs_df_person) <- tolower(names(acs_df_person))
acs_df_household[, "rt"] <- NULL
acs_df_person[, "rt"] <- NULL

acs_df <- merge(acs_df_household, acs_df_person)

stopifnot(nrow(acs_df) == nrow(acs_df_person))

acs_df[, "one"] <- 1

dim(acs_df)
##Build Survey Design
library(survey)

acs_design <-
  svrepdesign(
    weight = ~pwgtp,
    repweights = "pwgtp[0-9]+",
    scale = 4 / 80,
    rscales = rep(1, 80),
    mse = TRUE,
    type = "JK1",
    data = acs_df
  )

dim(acs_design)
##Prepare Variables
acs_design <- update(
  acs_design,
  pincp = as.numeric(pincp),
  agep  = as.numeric(agep)
)

design_e2 <- subset(
  acs_design,
  !is.na(pincp) & !is.na(agep)
)

sum(weights(design_e2, "sampling") != 0)

##The complete-case sample size for Estimator 2 is 87,189.

##Plain Weighted Mean
mean_fit <- svymean(~pincp, design_e2, na.rm = TRUE)

mean_est   <- coef(mean_fit)[1]
mean_se    <- SE(mean_fit)[1]
mean_bound <- 2 * mean_se
mean_ci    <- c(mean_est - mean_bound, mean_est + mean_bound)

mean_results <- data.frame(
  estimator = "Plain weighted mean",
  estimate  = mean_est,
  SE        = mean_se,
  bound     = mean_bound,
  CI_lower  = mean_ci[1],
  CI_upper  = mean_ci[2]
)

mean_results

##The plain weighted mean estimate is $46,206.98, with bound 527.36 and approximate 95% confidence interval (45,679.62, 46,734.34).

##Population Mean Age
xbar_fit <- svymean(~agep, acs_design, na.rm = TRUE)
Xbar_pop <- coef(xbar_fit)[1]

Xbar_pop

##The estimated population mean age is 40.61.

##Ratio Estimator
ratio_fit <- svyratio(~pincp, ~agep, design = design_e2)

ratio_R    <- coef(ratio_fit)[1]
ratio_se_R <- SE(ratio_fit)[1]

ratio_est   <- Xbar_pop * ratio_R
ratio_se    <- Xbar_pop * ratio_se_R
ratio_bound <- 2 * ratio_se
ratio_ci    <- c(ratio_est - ratio_bound, ratio_est + ratio_bound)

ratio_results <- data.frame(
  estimator = "Ratio estimator",
  estimate  = ratio_est,
  SE        = ratio_se,
  bound     = ratio_bound,
  CI_lower  = ratio_ci[1],
  CI_upper  = ratio_ci[2]
)

ratio_results

##The ratio estimator gives an estimated mean income of $39,489.99, with bound 457.09 and approximate 95% confidence interval (39,032.90, 39,947.08).

##Regression Estimator
reg_fit <- svyglm(pincp ~ agep, design = design_e2)

summary(reg_fit)
reg_pred <- predict(
  reg_fit,
  newdata = data.frame(agep = Xbar_pop),
  se = TRUE
)

reg_est <- as.numeric(reg_pred)
reg_se  <- sqrt(as.numeric(attr(reg_pred, "var")))

reg_bound <- 2 * reg_se
reg_ci    <- c(reg_est - reg_bound, reg_est + reg_bound)

reg_results <- data.frame(
  estimator = "Regression estimator",
  estimate  = reg_est,
  SE        = reg_se,
  bound     = reg_bound,
  CI_lower  = reg_ci[1],
  CI_upper  = reg_ci[2]
)

reg_results

##The regression estimator gives an estimated mean income of $43,555.05, with bound 506.95 and approximate 95% confidence interval (43,048.11, 44,062.00).

##Compare Estimators
all_results <- rbind(
  mean_results,
  ratio_results,
  reg_results
)

all_results

##The ratio estimator has the smallest standard error, but the regression estimator is preferred because the relationship between income and age is not expected to pass through the origin. The regression estimator therefore provides the main estimator for Estimator 2.

##Required Sample Size
B_target <- 1000

reg_resid <- residuals(reg_fit, type = "response")
reg_wts   <- weights(design_e2, type = "sampling")

Se2_hat <- sum(reg_wts * reg_resid^2, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(reg_wts, na.rm = TRUE)

n_required_no_fpc <- ceiling(4 * Se2_hat / (B_target^2))

N_pop <- 10037261

n_required_fpc <- ceiling(
  (4 * N_pop * Se2_hat) / (B_target^2 * N_pop + 4 * Se2_hat)
)

sample_size_results <- data.frame(
  target_bound = B_target,
  residual_variance = Se2_hat,
  n_required_no_fpc = n_required_no_fpc,
  n_required_fpc = n_required_fpc
)

sample_size_results

##Using a target bound of 1000, the estimated required sample size is 15,257 without finite population correction and 15,234 with finite population correction. Since the current sample size is 87,189, the available ACS sample is much larger than required.

##Conclusion

##For Estimator 2, the plain weighted mean estimate is $46,206.98, the ratio estimate is $39,489.99, and the regression estimate is $43,555.05. We prefer the regression estimator because it uses age as an auxiliary variable without imposing a proportional-through-origin relationship.
